description: Thai exhibits tone sandhi where the low lexical tone (21 or 11) changes to a rising tone (213 or 14) when followed by a high tone (45) or rising tone (214) in connected speech. This is an allophonic process that maintains tonal contrast while facilitating smooth pitch transitions between words.
sources: Gandour, J. T. (1978). _The perception and production of tones in Thai_. In _Proceedings of the VIIth International Congress of Phonetic Sciences_ (pp. 410-413).
Ladd, D. R. (2008). _Intonational Phonology_ (2nd ed.). Cambridge University Press.
Maddieson, I. (2005). _Tone in Thai: A new analysis_. In _Proceedings of the 13th International Congress of Phonetic Sciences_ (pp. 1-4).

languageExamples.orthography: มา (maa) + 確認 (kham) => มา ama
